editor.dragReady: Drag this file to Discord, Telegram, or a folder

sidebar.tip: Tip: You can customize your shortcuts from the settings page.

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.comingSoonDesc: Device management is under construction. Mouse, keyboard, and RGB configuration will be available in a future release.
dashboard.gsrWarning: GPU Screen Recorder is not installed. Install gpu-screen-recorder to enable replay buffer recording.
dashboard.earBlastOn: Ear Blast Protection ON — auto-lowers loud audio
dashboard.earBlastOff: Enable Ear Blast Protection (auto-lowers loud audio)

items: Per-app volume popover menu with live fill gradient and mute/unmute
Auto-hide timers on volume menu and context menu
Muted-state dimming for 0% volume apps
VU tick labels moved to left of track
Rounded window corners in non-fullscreen mode
Fixed 'Start on Boot' .desktop file fields
Fixed 'Minimize to Tray' close button behavior
Dashboard Quick Access — popovers for Mixer, Recorder, Clips, and Devices
Virtual Mic Routing — mic strip with noise-reduction toggle and per-app routing
Global Recording Dropdown — replay buffer controls in Clips page header
PipeWire routing leak fix — no more dual-device audio bleed
Smooth 60 Hz VU meters with attack/release ballistics
Overdrive mode — faders extend to 150%
System tray with background-run and autostart support
Crash log capture → ~/.local/share/opengg/opengg_crash.log
Advanced timeline editor with per-track filters
FFmpeg export pipeline with real-time progress
Waveform preview in clip editor
Dark theme v2 — #16213E base, accent-driven borders
Lazy clip grid rendering via Intersection Observer
Mic input strip with NC toggle
Per-app volume sliders in drop zones
i18n scaffold (Arabic + English)
GPU Screen Recorder integration — NVENC / VAAPI replay buffer
Global hotkey support for instant replay save
Clip gallery with thumbnail grid and metadata
Full Arabic localization with RTL layout support
Theme engine — dark/light mode with custom accent colors
Vue i18n integration with locale persistence
Device manager scaffold — ratbagd D-Bus mouse detection
OpenRGB SDK integration for unified RGB control
Game profile auto-switching foundation
Clip editor scaffold with custom video player
Thumbnail generation via FFmpeg probes
Clip metadata storage with SQLite backend
Dark theme v1 — surface/card/deep layering
Sidebar navigation with page routing
Settings page with persistence layer
Initial PipeWire mixer — 5-channel virtual sinks
Per-app audio routing via pw-metadata
Basic volume faders and mute toggles
Tauri 2 + Vue 3 scaffold
Custom titlebar with window controls
Project bootstrap and architecture setup

dashboard.moreUpdates: no more updates | {n} more update | {n} more updates

general.title: General
general.dateFormatHint: Format used when searching clips by date (e.g. YYYY-MM-DD).

language.title: Language
language.hint: Changes take effect immediately. RTL languages are fully supported.
language.rtlModeHint: Switches the entire interface to right-to-left layout, including the sidebar position.

shortcuts.title: Shortcuts
shortcuts.hint: Click a binding to record a new shortcut. Press Escape to cancel.

hints.saveReplay: Saves the current replay buffer as a new clip file
hints.toggleRecording: Starts or stops a manual full-length recording session
hints.screenshot: Captures a screenshot of the active screen to your Pictures folder
hints.toggleEarBlast: Toggles Ear Blast Protection on or off
hints.splitClip: Splits the clip at the current playhead position in the editor
hints.exportClip: Opens the export dialog to save the trimmed clip as a video file
hints.toggleMic: Mutes or unmutes your microphone across all recording tracks
hints.undo: Undo the last trim change in the editor
hints.redo: Redo the last undone trim change

earBlast.title: Ear Blast Protection
earBlast.desc: Automatically reduces volume when audio exceeds the trigger threshold. Restores original volume when levels normalize.

captureSound.title: Capture & Sound
captureSound.captureHint: Assign PipeWire virtual sinks to each recording track (OBS-style).

storage.title: Storage
storage.mediaDirsHint: Clip Directories are watched for new recordings. Screenshot Directories are where editor screenshots are saved.
storage.steamBody: Add your Steam library to OpenGG's game pickers and keep it visible here in Storage. After you allow access, OpenGG reads local Steam data and may request Steam Store metadata for names and artwork.
storage.steamDeniedBody: Allow access to read local Steam data and request Steam Store metadata so OpenGG can list your Steam library here with names and artwork.
storage.steamReadyBody: Your Steam library is available in game pickers and listed here with artwork where available. OpenGG uses local Steam data plus Steam Store metadata for names and images.

extensions.title: Extensions
extensions.hint: Enable or disable extensions. Disabled extensions are hidden everywhere — keeping the interface clean for what you actually use.
extensions.noExtensions: No extensions found. Drop an extension folder with a manifest.json into the extensions directory.
extensions.restartHint: Changes apply immediately — no restart required.

store.title: Extension Store
store.comingSoonDesc: Browse and install community extensions directly from OpenGG. This feature is under development.

daemon.title: Daemon & Startup
daemon.runAtStartupTooltip: Automatically launch OpenGG and its background services when you log in.
daemon.keepInBackgroundTooltip: Closing the main window will hide it to the system tray instead of quitting the application.

diagnostics.title: Diagnostics
diagnostics.hint: Crash and error logs are stored locally for debugging. Share them when reporting issues.

dangerZone.title: Danger Zone
dangerZone.subtitle: These actions are destructive. Use only if your audio routing is broken and needs a full reset.
dangerZone.removeVirtualAudio: Remove Virtual Audio & Restore OS Defaults
dangerZone.removeVirtualAudioDesc: Unloads all OpenGG virtual sinks/sources without restarting PipeWire or WirePlumber. Your physical hardware routes will be restored. The onboarding wizard will guide re-setup on next launch.
dangerZone.confirmMsg: This will unload all OpenGG virtual audio sinks. Other audio (browser, games) will keep playing. Continue?
dangerZone.resetVirtualAudioDesc: Remove and recreate the virtual audio engine.
dangerZone.createVirtualAudioDesc: Set up OpenGG's virtual sinks so you can route Game, Chat, Media, and Aux independently.
dangerZone.createConfirmMsg: This will create virtual audio sinks for independent channel routing. Continue?

timelineTracks.title: Timeline Tracks
timelineTracks.trackListHint: Customize the name, color, and icon for each editor timeline track. Changes apply live — open the editor to see them instantly.
timelineTracks.livePreviewHint: Exactly how the editor timeline headers will look. Updates as you type.
timelineTracks.visibilityTooltip: Toggle track visibility in Live Preview

captureGsr.title: GPU Screen Recorder
captureGsr.hint: Uses gpu-screen-recorder for low-latency hardware-encoded replay buffer (NVENC/VAAPI). Must be installed separately.
captureGsr.extensionsHint: Enable GPU Screen Recorder in Extensions to configure it here.
captureGsr.autoStartTooltip: Automatically start the replay buffer when OpenGG opens.
captureGsr.waylandHint: Wayland session detected. "Fullscreen Application" capture requires X11.
captureGsr.diagnosticsOk: All checks passed — ready to record.
captureGsr.diagnosticsFail: Some checks failed. Fix the issues below before recording.
captureGsr.autoRestartTooltip: Automatically restart the replay buffer if gpu-screen-recorder crashes unexpectedly.
captureGsr.errorMissingGroups: Your user is not in the 'render' group. Run: sudo usermod -aG render,video $USER — then re-login.
captureGsr.errorBinaryUnhealthy: gpu-screen-recorder is installed but failed to run. Check that it is a compatible version and your GPU drivers are up to date.
captureGsr.crashWarning: Recording stopped unexpectedly. Check logs for details.

notificationsPage.title: Notifications
notificationsPage.description: Configure how OpenGG notifies you when clips are saved or recording events occur.
notificationsPage.positionDesc: Where on screen the notification appears.
notificationsPage.durationDesc: How long the notification stays visible (in seconds).

aboutPage.tagline: Your ultimate open-source Linux gaming companion.
aboutPage.description: OpenGG is a modular hub designed to bring professional-grade gaming tools to the Linux ecosystem.
aboutPage.goal_1: Provide a lightweight, dependency-free alternative to SteelSeries GG on Linux
aboutPage.goal_2: Deliver professional-grade audio routing with PipeWire and per-app volume control
aboutPage.goal_3: Enable seamless clip capture with gpu-screen-recorder integration and instant replay
aboutPage.contributors: OpenGG is maintained by the community. Contributions are welcome!

deps.title: System Dependencies
deps.missingFfmpeg: ffmpeg not found — export and thumbnails disabled.

clips.noClipsHint: Try again or adjust your filters
clips.noClipsEmptyHint: Start recording some or import them if you have any

steamImport.title: Import your Steam library
steamImport.body: Bring your Steam library into the existing game filters and tag pickers. After you allow it, OpenGG reads local Steam data and may request Steam Store metadata for game names and artwork.
steamImport.deniedBody: OpenGG has not imported your Steam library. Allow access to read local Steam data and request Steam Store metadata for game names and icons.
steamImport.readyBody: Your Steam library is available in the game filters and clip game pickers, with artwork where available. OpenGG uses local Steam data plus Steam Store metadata for names and images.
steamImport.consentMessage: Allow OpenGG to read your local Steam data and request Steam Store metadata to populate your Steam library, game names, and artwork?

devices.title: Devices
devices.noDevicesDesc: Connect a supported mouse or headset and click Refresh
devices.macroNoButton: Select a button on the mouse diagram
devices.comingSoonDesc: We are working on providing full support for your favourite devices. Stay tuned for upcoming updates.

access.title: Device Access Setup Required
access.description: Some device features need system permissions to work properly. Run the setup command to configure your system.
access.runSetup: Run this command in a terminal:

welcome.title: Welcome to OpenGG
welcome.desc: Your open-source Linux gaming hub. Let's take a quick tour of what you can do.

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.desc: Your command center. Quick-access cards for Audio, Recording, Clips, and Devices. Click any card for fast actions.

mixer.title: Audio Mixer
mixer.desc: Route Game, Chat, Media, and Aux through independent virtual sinks. Drag apps between channels and control per-app volume.

clips.title: Clips & Recording
clips.desc: Save instant replays, browse your clip gallery, and import folders. The replay buffer runs in the background while you play.

scanning.title: Scanning
scanning.desc: Import entire folders of clips with one click. Device scanning will be available in a future update.

settings.title: Settings
settings.desc: Customize themes, shortcuts, and recording quality. Set up virtual audio in the Audio Engine tab.

finish.title: You're Ready!
finish.desc: If you haven't set up virtual audio yet, we'll guide you through it now.

step1.title: Virtual Audio Engine Required
step1.description: OpenGG uses virtual audio sinks to route Game, Chat, Media, and Aux channels independently. This one-time setup takes about 2 seconds.

step2.title: Link Your Devices
step2.description: Select which physical devices the mixer should use by default. You can change these anytime in the Mixer.
